title: How to Get Crypto Payments for Tanzanian Online Sellers Live Before the End of the Day
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: You're a Tanzanian digital creator stuck with a PayPal or Stripe account that won't let you sell your ebooks, courses, or art. Your customers in the US or Europe can't afford the high fees or simply can't use those platforms. You've tried every other option but no one seems to want to take a chance on you. By the end of the day, you need to have crypto payments live on your website so you can get paid in the currency of your choice. You can do it, but you need the right tools and a clear plan.
What You Can Actually Get Done Today

You can set up a cryptocurrency payment system that doesn't require your customers to create an account or go through a lengthy verification process. You can accept payments in multiple coins and send automated receipts to your customers. You can integrate this system with your existing website using a simple plugin or a custom solution. To get started, you'll need to choose a cryptocurrency payment processor that supports the coins you want to accept and has a good reputation. This might be a bit overwhelming, but you can do it today if you focus on the minimum viable setup that will get you live.
The Minimum Viable Setup That Works

The minimum viable setup that works involves integrating a cryptocurrency payment processor with a wallet system that can handle multiple coins. This setup will allow you to accept payments and send automated receipts to your customers without them having to create an account or go through a lengthy verification process. You can use a plugin like Web3 Wallet Connect to integrate a wallet system with your website. This plugin is available for a one-time fee of $19.99 and includes features like multi-chain QR codes, live price feeds, and support for over 300 coins. With this setup, you can get live in a matter of hours.
Skipping the Steps That Do Not Matter Yet

There are many steps involved in setting up a cryptocurrency payment system, but some of them don't matter yet. You don't need to worry about things like security audits or regulatory compliance just yet. What you need to focus on is getting live and selling your products to your customers. You can always come back to these details later. For now, focus on getting your minimum viable setup working and then iterate from there.
You can get live with cryptocurrency payments today without needing to create an account or go through a lengthy verification process.
Getting Your First Transaction Live

To get your first transaction live, you'll need to test your payment system and make sure it's working correctly. This involves sending a test transaction and verifying that it was received correctly. You'll also need to make sure that your wallet system is properly configured and that you have the necessary API keys to integrate with the payment processor. Once you've completed these steps, you can start accepting payments from your customers and getting live.
What to Do in the Next 24 Hours

In the next 24 hours, you should focus on getting your payment system fully integrated with your website and testing it thoroughly. This involves making sure that everything is working correctly and that your customers can use it easily. You should also make sure that you have a plan in place for handling customer support and resolving any issues that may arise. By the end of the next 24 hours, you should have a fully functional payment system that you can rely on to get paid for your work.
